description: Mandatory safety and regulatory decals required by most sanctioning bodies. SPB warns the user if any of these zones are missing on a finished livery.

schema.name: string — human label
schema.color_rule: string — required color or contrast rule

decals.name: Driver Name Plate
decals.placement: Roof rail, A-pillar, or above driver door
decals.color_rule: Must contrast with body color — black on light, white on dark
decals.notes: Driver identification for trackside marshals, broadcast graphics, and emergency response. Some series mandate the name above the door, others on the roof rail. Length varies with name — leave at least 15% UV width.

decals.name: Blood Type Indicator
decals.placement: A-pillar, helmet, or roll cage area
decals.color_rule: Red text on white background, square or shield-shaped
decals.notes: Critical for emergency medical response. Shows driver's ABO and Rh blood type so medics can begin transfusion preparation before transport. Must be visible from outside the cabin without opening doors.

decals.name: Sim-Series Nameplate
decals.color_rule: Series-defined — usually white text on dark background
decals.notes: Sim-racing equivalent of the driver nameplate. Required for league participation and broadcast eligibility. Some sim platforms generate this dynamically; others require it baked into the livery.

decals.name: Class Indicator Light Surround
decals.placement: Roof corner, rear deck, front fender
decals.color_rule: Black surround around the LED — does not paint the LED itself
decals.notes: Multi-class endurance racing requires illuminated class indicator lights (yellow GTD, red GTP, etc.). The decal is the bezel that contrasts the LED, not the light itself. SPB should reserve these zones from livery paint to avoid covering the LEDs.

decals.name: Transponder Mount Marker
decals.placement: Lower nose, side skirt, or undertray edge
decals.color_rule: Yellow with black border — high-vis utility marker
decals.notes: Marks the transponder mounting location for tech inspection. Transponders are series-issued; the decal indicates where the unit is fastened. Often overlooked on custom liveries — SPB should flag if missing.

decals.name: Pit Window / Fuel Cell Marker
decals.placement: Rear quarter near fuel filler, or roof above filler
decals.color_rule: High-contrast — usually red or yellow on white background
decals.notes: Locates the fuel cell filler for pit crew and emergency responders. May also indicate the safe pit window for refueling. Mandatory in most refueling-allowed series. Must remain visible regardless of livery design.
